# SUBMISSIONS — the ecosystem publish kit (GAP-B, generated by tools/publish.py)

Constitution Art. I §1: this factory never opens PRs/issues on third-party
repos. Everything below is PREPARED; the operator clicks. Each target's own
intended intake is used, nothing else. Regenerate: `python3 tools/publish.py`.

## Target 0 — passive indexers (already live)
`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` is valid and versioned (the validator law);
community crawlers index marketplaces from that file automatically. Nothing to do.

## Target 1 — awesome-claude-code (submission via THEIR issue intake)
Repo: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code — use their current submission form if one exists;
the prefilled link below fills a plain issue with the same content.

[Prefilled submission issue](https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/issues/new?title=Submission%3A%20Nightshift%20Foundry%20%E2%80%94%20an%20autonomous%20plugin%20foundry%20%2B%20marketplace&body=%2A%2AMarketplace%3A%2A%2A%20%60/plugin%20marketplace%20add%20GhostlyGawd/plugin-foundry%60%0A%2A%2ALive%20catalog%3A%2A%2A%20https%3A//ghostlygawd.github.io/plugin-foundry%0A%2A%2AWhat%20it%20is%3A%2A%2A%20an%20autonomous%20Claude%20Code%20plugin%20foundry%20%E2%80%94%20the%20repo%20invents%2C%20builds%2C%20QAs%2C%20and%20ships%20single-job%20plugins%20on%20a%20loop%2C%20governed%20by%20a%20written%20constitution%20and%20a%20single-writer%20orchestrator%3B%20every%20plugin%20has%20a%20public%20birth%20certificate%20%28tested%20%2B%20reviewed%20before%20publish%29.%0A%0A%2A%2AThe%20shelf%20right%20now%3A%2A%2A%0A-%20%60plugin-smith%60%20v0.1.3%20%E2%80%94%20Scaffolds%20new%20Claude%20Code%20plugins%20and%20health-checks%20existing%20ones%20against%20the%20official%20spec.%0A-%20%60fork-a-foundry%60%20v0.2.0%20%E2%80%94%20One%20skill%20that%20bootstraps%20your%20own%20self-running%20plugin%20workshop%20%E2%80%94%20the%20factory%2C%20shipping%20itself.%0A-%20%60commit-craft%60%20v0.3.2%20%E2%80%94%20Drafts%20conventional%20commits%20from%20the%20staged%20diff%20and%20guards%20message%20format%20at%20commit%20time.%0A-%20%60session-recap%60%20v0.2.0%20%E2%80%94%20Writes%20a%20structured%20recap%20when%20a%20session%20ends%20and%20recalls%20it%20when%20the%20next%20one%20starts.%0A-%20%60env-doctor%60%20v0.2.0%20%E2%80%94%20Checks%20the%20toolchain%20against%20the%20project%27s%20declared%20versions%20at%20session%20start%20and%20warns%20early.%0A-%20%60pr-narrator%60%20v0.1.3%20%E2%80%94%20A%20skill%20that%20turns%20a%20branch%27s%20commits%20and%20diff%20into%20a%20reviewer-ready%20PR%20description.%0A-%20%60night-clerk%60%20v0.2.7%20%E2%80%94%20Ask%20Claude%20Code%20what%20plugin%20helps%20with%20X%20-%20the%20clerk%20searches%20the%20foundry%20catalog%20and%20hands%20you%20the%20install%20line.%0A-%20%60test-gap-nudge%60%20v0.3.1%20%E2%80%94%20A%20polite%20Stop-hook%20that%20notices%20source%20changes%20with%20no%20matching%20test%20changes%20and%20says%20so%20once.%0A-%20%60dep-bump-brief%60%20v0.1.1%20%E2%80%94%20Turns%20a%20dependency-bump%20diff%20into%20a%20plain-language%20brief%20%E2%80%94%20what%20changed%2C%20what%20might%20break%2C%20what%20to%20check.%0A-%20%60todo-ledger%60%20v0.1.0%20%E2%80%94%20Inventories%20TODO/FIXME/HACK%20comments%20with%20git-blame%20age%20and%20owners%20%E2%80%94%20tech%20debt%20as%20a%20dated%2C%20ranked%20ledger.)

## Target 2 — Anthropic community marketplace listing
Docs + intake: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins
Submit the marketplace repo `GhostlyGawd/plugin-foundry` through the form the docs point to.
This is the single biggest credibility unlock (MASTER §11).

## Target 3 — the copy block (any directory that takes a blurb)
```
Nightshift Foundry — the autonomous software company you can fork.
An AI-run plugin workshop in a repo: it invents, builds, tests, reviews,
and ships Claude Code plugins on a loop — governed by a constitution,
a guard, and a single-writer orchestrator. Every number it shows is
computed from its own records.
  /plugin marketplace add GhostlyGawd/plugin-foundry
  https://ghostlygawd.github.io/plugin-foundry
```

## Per-plugin blurbs (for individual listings)

- **Plugin Smith** (`/plugin install plugin-smith@foundry`, v0.1.3, meta) — Scaffolds new Claude Code plugins and health-checks existing ones against the official spec.
- **Fork a Foundry** (`/plugin install fork-a-foundry@foundry`, v0.2.0, meta) — One skill that bootstraps your own self-running plugin workshop — the factory, shipping itself.
- **Commit Craft** (`/plugin install commit-craft@foundry`, v0.3.2, workflow) — Drafts conventional commits from the staged diff and guards message format at commit time.
- **Session Recap** (`/plugin install session-recap@foundry`, v0.2.0, context) — Writes a structured recap when a session ends and recalls it when the next one starts.
- **Env Doctor** (`/plugin install env-doctor@foundry`, v0.2.0, quality) — Checks the toolchain against the project's declared versions at session start and warns early.
- **PR Narrator** (`/plugin install pr-narrator@foundry`, v0.1.3, workflow) — A skill that turns a branch's commits and diff into a reviewer-ready PR description.
- **Night Clerk** (`/plugin install night-clerk@foundry`, v0.2.7, workflow) — Ask Claude Code what plugin helps with X - the clerk searches the foundry catalog and hands you the install line.
- **Test-Gap Nudge** (`/plugin install test-gap-nudge@foundry`, v0.3.1, quality) — A polite Stop-hook that notices source changes with no matching test changes and says so once.
- **Dep-Bump Brief** (`/plugin install dep-bump-brief@foundry`, v0.1.1, workflow) — Turns a dependency-bump diff into a plain-language brief — what changed, what might break, what to check.
- **TODO Ledger** (`/plugin install todo-ledger@foundry`, v0.1.0, quality) — Inventories TODO/FIXME/HACK comments with git-blame age and owners — tech debt as a dated, ranked ledger.
